executive_summary: Treasury is the Commonwealth's lead economic adviser — a policy-department archetype where demand sits in governance, evaluation, workforce, data/digital and assurance rather than large service-delivery programs. The dominant theme for 2025-26 is absorbing significant machinery-of-government changes (housing supply, homelessness, construction, cities and planning policy embedded into Treasury via the 13 May 2025 AAO), which creates operating-model, workforce and performance-framework demand. The plan signals deliberate uplift in data/AI capability (a second Enterprise Data Strategy, a refreshed Digital and Cyber Security Strategy), an evaluation push through the Australian Centre for Evaluation, and continued embedding of a risk framework and pro-integrity culture. External signals reinforce this: ANAO recommendations on gifts/benefits, an in-progress HAFF audit, and a Measuring What Matters framework audit. Note the plan commits to bringing some ICT/digital work in-house under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework, tempering pure build opportunities.

strategic_priorities.title: Integrate new housing and related functions
strategic_priorities.description: Treasury must operationalise a range of new housing functions (plus homelessness, construction, cities and planning policy) embedded through 2025 machinery-of-government changes, and deliver the National Housing Accord with Housing Australia.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We will have a particular focus on delivering more integrated advice and implementation of the government's housing policy agenda, reflecting the range of new housing functions that have been embedded into Treasury.

strategic_priorities.title: Streamline and strengthen the foreign investment framework
strategic_priorities.description: Treasury is moving to a risk-based foreign investment model — faster decisions on low-risk proposals (50% within 30 days), more scrutiny of high-risk proposals, and stronger compliance monitoring and enforcement.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Treasury will strengthen its risk-based approach to assessing foreign investment proposals... streamlining the approvals of low-risk investments, increase scrutiny of high-risk investments, and bolster compliance monitoring and enforcement activities.

strategic_priorities.title: Uplift data, digital, AI and cyber capability
strategic_priorities.description: Treasury will refresh its 2024-26 Digital and Cyber Security Strategy, launch a second Enterprise Data Strategy, and integrate AI guidance and governance into existing strategies in 2025-26.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: During 2025-26, Treasury will develop and launch its second Enterprise Data Strategy... Across 2025-26, Treasury will integrate guidance on, and oversight of, AI into its existing strategies and governance arrangements.

strategic_priorities.title: Build a service-wide evaluation capability
strategic_priorities.description: Through the Australian Centre for Evaluation, Treasury will release the Treasury Evaluation Strategy and oversee a service-wide evaluation capability uplift to embed evidence in decision-making.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: In 2025-26, we will release the Treasury Evaluation Strategy, which will complement and add value to our existing evidence and assurance ecosystem.

strategic_priorities.title: Embed risk management and pro-integrity culture
strategic_priorities.description: Treasury will continue embedding its risk framework (finalised late 2024), integrate climate risk into governance, and embed the Integrity Strategy 2024-2028 with a focus on conflict-of-interest management.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We will strengthen our risk management by integrating risk with other governance and strategic planning activities.

strategic_priorities.title: Reform the Payment Times Reporting Scheme
strategic_priorities.description: Treasury is overhauling the Payment Times Reporting Scheme following the statutory review, with new reporting requirements, IT system and a re-established compliance target.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: continuing to improve payment times to small businesses through the effective administration of the recently reformed Payment Times Reporting Scheme.

strategic_priorities.title: Refresh the corporate performance framework
strategic_priorities.description: Treasury introduced three new performance measures for 2025-26 and will review the framework for completeness to incorporate transferred functions, potentially varying the Corporate Plan.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Treasury has introduced three new performance measures for 2025-26 to replace ceasing measures and expand on the performance reporting of our regulatory functions.

reform_and_change_drivers: Machinery-of-government changes (13 May 2025 AAO) embedding housing, homelessness, construction, cities and planning functions into Treasury
Government housing agenda: National Housing Accord, Housing Australia Future Fund (40,000 dwellings to 2029), National Housing Supply and Affordability Council
Foreign investment framework reform with new 30-day decision target and risk-based triage from 1 January 2025
Statutory review response overhauling the Payment Times Reporting Scheme, including a new IT system
Future Made in Australia: National Interest Framework, Investor Front Door pilot, net zero and economic-resilience industrial priorities
Net zero transition: whole-of-economy modelling for the Net Zero Plan and Sustainable Finance Roadmap
Economic Reform Roundtable focused on productivity, resilience and budget sustainability
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework driving in-housing of some ICT and digital core work
Commonwealth Climate Risk and Opportunity Management Program requiring enterprise climate risk assessment
Rapid AI adoption requiring governance, data capability and responsible-use frameworks
challenges_and_risks: Absorbing new functions and staff through MoG changes while maintaining advice quality (named staff capability and culture risks)
Modelling and analytical capability risk in a rapidly changing economic environment including net zero transition
Housing policy and program delivery risk using novel partnership delivery arrangements
IT systems, cyber security, governance and capability risk (very low to medium tolerance)
Integrity and conflict-of-interest exposure, reinforced by an ANAO gifts/benefits audit with four agreed recommendations
Performance framework gaps as new measures bed in and transferred functions are incorporated
Productivity and budget-sustainability pressures shaping the policy workload
Weak/maturing regulator stakeholder satisfaction (65% target retained after consecutive misses)

news_and_controversies.summary: FOI-released documents showed Treasury initially declined to act on internal complaints about the Australian Office of Financial Management (which manages ~A$1 trillion in borrowing), then reversed course weeks later and ordered an independent review.
news_and_controversies.implication: Signals live demand for independent governance/assurance reviews and internal-complaints handling within the portfolio — a credible entry point for governance and integrity advisory.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO found Treasury 'largely effective' in complying with gifts, benefits and hospitality requirements but identified shortcomings in policy alignment with APSC requirements, training for statutory office holders, and detection processes; four recommendations were made and all agreed.
anao_findings.implication: Open remediation runway in integrity controls, policy alignment and APS-conduct training design — discrete, scoped advisory work.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO Auditor-General Report No. 34 of 2024-25 assessed the effectiveness of Treasury's design and implementation of the Measuring What Matters national wellbeing framework released in July 2023.
anao_findings.implication: Scrutiny of Treasury's measurement/evaluation capability — potential demand for performance-measurement, data and framework-implementation support.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO has an in-progress performance audit assessing the effectiveness of Treasury's design and delivery of the Housing Australia Future Fund.
anao_findings.implication: An imminent audit on a flagship program; pre- and post-audit assurance, program-design and delivery-remediation work is likely to follow.

anao_findings.summary: In the ANAO's 2022-23 annual performance statements audits, Treasury received an unmodified (clean) conclusion while four other departments were qualified.
anao_findings.implication: Treasury's reporting baseline is comparatively sound, so opportunities lean toward continuous improvement rather than crisis remediation.

parliamentary_matters.summary: The Treasury portfolio was examined across recent Senate Economics Legislation Committee estimates rounds, including 2023-24 Additional Estimates, 2024-25 Budget Estimates and 2024-25 Supplementary Budget Estimates (with the then-Secretary tabling an opening statement).
parliamentary_matters.implication: Predictable, high-frequency scrutiny cycle drives demand for estimates preparation, briefing/QON support and reputational-risk readiness; aligning offerings to this calendar helps.

opportunity_areas.area: Integrating new housing and related functions into Treasury
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Treasury must build an operating model and governance for newly transferred housing, homelessness, construction, cities and planning functions while sustaining advice quality.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A target operating model and governance design for the integrated housing/cities/planning functions — mapping accountabilities, decision rights and committee oversight, with a transition roadmap. Pair with Organisation, Workforce & People for the workforce/change dimension.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The Secretary explicitly flags 'integrated advice and implementation' of new housing functions and the 13 May 2025 AAO transfers; the plan names 'state office planning' and integrating new responsibilities as workforce priorities.

opportunity_areas.area: Independent assurance of the Housing Australia Future Fund delivery
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Treasury is accountable for HAFF/National Housing Accord delivery (40,000 dwellings to 2029) using 'novel partnership delivery arrangements' carrying a high-tolerance program risk, and faces an in-progress ANAO audit.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent, gateway-style delivery assurance of the housing program — health-check of governance, milestones and benefits realisation ahead of and in response to the ANAO HAFF audit.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Plan names housing policy and program risk with high tolerance and 'novel partnership delivery arrangements'; reinforced by the ANAO in-progress HAFF performance audit.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Treasury is embedding its Integrity Strategy 2024-2028 with a focus on conflict-of-interest identification and reporting, and must close four agreed ANAO gifts/benefits recommendations.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A targeted integrity health-check and remediation plan addressing the ANAO gifts/benefits recommendations — policy-to-APSC alignment, detection processes and conflict-of-interest controls, with Strategy, Policy & Governance support on framework design.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Plan states a focus on 'strengthening conflict of interest identification, management, and reporting'; the ANAO audit made four agreed recommendations creating a clear remediation runway.

opportunity_areas.area: Evaluation capability and Treasury Evaluation Strategy
opportunity_areas.agency_need: The Australian Centre for Evaluation is leading a service-wide evaluation capability uplift and Treasury will release its Evaluation Strategy in 2025-26; the MWM framework was ANAO-audited.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Design support for the Treasury Evaluation Strategy rollout and process/outcome evaluations of priority programs, with Data, Analytics & Information as secondary for the evidence base.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Plan commits to releasing the Treasury Evaluation Strategy and a service-wide evaluation uplift; the ANAO MWM audit signals scrutiny of Treasury's measurement capability.

opportunity_areas.area: Enterprise Data Strategy and AI governance
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Treasury will launch a second Enterprise Data Strategy and integrate AI guidance and oversight into its strategies and governance in 2025-26.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An information/data strategy and governance engagement — data governance and culture design supporting the Enterprise Data Strategy, plus an AI-use governance framework integrated with existing risk arrangements.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Plan explicitly commits to developing the second Enterprise Data Strategy and integrating AI governance; named IT/data capability risk supports the demand.

opportunity_areas.area: Digital and Cyber Security Strategy refresh
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Treasury will refresh its 2024-26 Digital and Cyber Security Strategy and manages an IT systems/cyber risk with very low to medium tolerance.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Cyber advisory to support the strategy refresh — a security posture assessment against current threats and whole-of-government initiatives, feeding the refreshed roadmap.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Plan commits to refreshing the Digital and Cyber Security Strategy and names cyber security among its capability risks. Tempered by the in-housing commitment under the Strategic Commissioning Framework.

opportunity_areas.area: Workforce planning and change management for MoG transition
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Treasury is implementing its Strategic Workforce Plan, supporting staff and leaders through change, position management and state office planning as new functions arrive.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Change-management and workforce-planning support for the MoG integration — leadership-through-change, position management/reporting uplift, and capability mapping for transferred functions.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Plan names 'supporting employees and leaders to manage through change', 'implementation of position management' and 'state office planning' as key efforts under the Workforce Plan.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Treasury must meet a 50%-within-30-days target by streamlining low-risk proposals and bolstering compliance monitoring, relying on a case-processing system.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Business/process analysis and service design of the foreign investment assessment workflow — triage redesign and case-system process improvements to lift on-time decision rates. Secondary: Data, Analytics & Information for risk-based decisioning.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Plan sets a new 50%/30-day efficiency target, references the case-processing system, and commits to streamlining low-risk and strengthening compliance — a measurable process challenge.

opportunity_areas.area: Performance framework redesign for transferred functions
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Treasury introduced three new measures and must review the framework for completeness to incorporate transferred functions, potentially varying the plan.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A PGPA-aligned performance-measure review and redesign covering transferred functions and regulator-performance reporting, ensuring measures meet PGPA Rule requirements.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Plan states new measures were introduced and the framework will be reviewed for completeness including transferred functions, with possible plan variation.

opportunity_areas.area: Risk framework maturation and climate risk integration
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Treasury is embedding its late-2024 risk framework, integrating risk with strategic planning and incorporating climate risk and opportunity reporting into governance.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A risk-framework maturity uplift integrating enterprise and climate risk into governance and planning, with Audit & Assurance support for committee-effectiveness review.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Plan commits to integrating risk with governance and strategic planning and building on the first enterprise climate risk assessment under the Commonwealth Climate Risk and Opportunity Management Program.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the Deputy Secretary / Group head responsible for the integrated housing, cities and planning functions with a target-operating-model and delivery-assurance proposition tied to the HAFF.
recommended_actions.rationale: MoG integration plus high-tolerance housing program risk and an in-progress ANAO HAFF audit create a clear, time-bound, multi-strand opportunity spanning governance, workforce and assurance.

recommended_actions.action: Offer the Chief Risk Officer / audit and risk committee a scoped integrity health-check addressing the four agreed ANAO gifts/benefits recommendations and conflict-of-interest controls.
recommended_actions.rationale: Agreed ANAO recommendations and the plan's explicit conflict-of-interest focus give a defined remediation runway with a willing internal sponsor.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short capability statement and thought piece on data governance and responsible AI adoption for the Chief Data/Information Officer ahead of the second Enterprise Data Strategy launch.
recommended_actions.rationale: The Enterprise Data Strategy and AI-governance integration are named 2025-26 deliverables; early positioning before the strategy lands is advantageous.

recommended_actions.action: Engage the Australian Centre for Evaluation lead on evaluation-design support as the Treasury Evaluation Strategy is released.
recommended_actions.rationale: A named 2025-26 deliverable plus the ANAO MWM audit make evaluation capability a live, evidence-backed need.

recommended_actions.action: Confirm whether the foreign investment case-processing and Payment Times Reporting IT work falls inside or outside the APS Strategic Commissioning in-housing scope before pitching digital delivery.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan commits to bringing some ICT/digital work in-house, so qualifying scope avoids pursuing work the department intends to do itself.

engagement_targets: Deputy Secretary / Group head for Housing (including transferred homelessness, construction, cities and planning functions)
Chief Operating Officer / Corporate Group (workforce, governance, state office planning)
Chief Risk Officer and the Audit and Risk Committee (integrity, risk framework, ANAO remediation)
Chief Data Officer / Chief Information Officer (Enterprise Data Strategy, AI governance, Digital and Cyber Security Strategy refresh)
Head of the Australian Centre for Evaluation (Treasury Evaluation Strategy)
Foreign Investment Division (case-processing and risk-based triage)
Performance / corporate planning function (PGPA performance-measure redesign)
talking_points: You are absorbing major new housing, cities and planning functions through the 13 May 2025 AAO — we can stand up a target operating model and transition plan so the integration doesn't dilute advice quality.
With the ANAO auditing the Housing Australia Future Fund, independent gateway-style delivery assurance positions you ahead of the findings rather than reacting to them.
The four agreed ANAO gifts/benefits recommendations and your conflict-of-interest focus map to a tight integrity health-check we can deliver in weeks, not months.
Your second Enterprise Data Strategy and AI-governance integration are the moment to embed data governance and responsible-AI controls — we can shape both as the strategy lands.
The Treasury Evaluation Strategy and the Measuring What Matters audit point to a service-wide evaluation uplift we can support with both framework design and program evaluations.
We understand the Strategic Commissioning Framework — our offer is advisory and assurance that complements your in-housing of core ICT, not competition with it.
caveats: This is a policy-department archetype, so opportunities are advisory/assurance/governance-weighted rather than large delivery programs — and the plan explicitly commits to bringing some ICT/digital work in-house under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework, which constrains pure build opportunities. The plan is high-level on internal initiatives (strategies named but not detailed), so several opportunities are inferred from named deliverables rather than evidenced procurements. External research was only partially completed: the News and Parliament categories are thin (the search tool hit a usage cap), so the AOFM news item and estimates cycle should be treated as indicative, not exhaustive. ANAO findings are well-sourced and are the strongest demand signals here.
